title: UNITAR Green Economic Acceleration Programme 2026 for ASEAN Professionals | Fully Funded Sustainable Green Finance Training in Japan
summary: The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) has officially launched applications for the Green Economic Acceleration: A Japan–ASEAN Strategic Programme for Sustainable Green Finance, a prestigious fully funded regional training programme designed to strengthen sustainable finance leadership and green economic transformation across ASEAN countries. Supported by the Government and the People of Japan, the programme will equip government officials, private-sector professionals, entrepreneurs, and sustainability leaders with practical knowledge and advanced skills in sustainable finance, green investment, carbon markets, energy transition, and climate-related economic development. The programme will run from July 2026 to March 2027 through a three-phase learning structure that combines online learning, advanced thematic training, expert mentoring, project development, and an international summit in Japan. Participation in the programme is completely free of charge. About the UNITAR Green Economic Acceleration Programme The Green Economic Acceleration Programme is a strategic Japan-ASEAN initiative created to strengthen regional cooperation in: Sustainable finance Green economic growth Climate resilience Clean energy investment Carbon market development Green infrastructure Environmental sustainability The programme aims to support professionals in ASEAN countries to develop practical, bankable green economy projects capable of driving sustainable development and regional economic resilience. Participants will engage in a highly interactive learning experience featuring: Self-paced online learning Expert-led sessions International peer exchange Mentorship opportunities Capstone project development Networking with Japanese institutions and industry leaders The programme is organized by UNITAR, the dedicated training arm of the United Nations, known globally for strengthening institutional and individual capacities through high-quality training and innovative learning initiatives. Programme Duration and Structure The programme will take place between: July 2026 – March 2027 It is divided into three progressively selective phases. Phase I: Online Learning Programme Duration: July to September 2026 The first phase is open to all accepted participants. During this stage, participants will complete self-paced online learning focused on: Sustainable finance Green economy systems Climate-related economic transformation Japan-ASEAN green cooperation Activities Include: Online learning modules Expert webinars Interactive peer discussions Participation in an online learning community Submission of an early-stage capstone project idea This phase is designed to provide foundational understanding and practical exposure to sustainable economic development strategies. Phase II: Advanced Thematic Training and Expert Mentoring Duration: October to December 2026 Only the top 200 performing participants from Phase I will advance to this stage. Participants will receive specialized advanced training in one of four major thematic tracks: Thematic Areas: Energy Transition Carbon Markets and Emission Trading Sustainable Banking and Green Fintech Green Transformation Policy Activities Include: Advanced expert-led training Peer-to-peer collaboration Mentorship from sustainability specialists Development of practical green finance projects Capstone refinement and strategic project planning This phase focuses heavily on helping participants transform their ideas into practical and investable green economy solutions. Phase III: Japan-ASEAN Green Investment Summit in Japan Date: February 2027 The top 50 participants from Phase II will be selected to travel to Japan for the prestigious Japan-ASEAN Green Investment Summit. This phase provides participants with international exposure and direct engagement opportunities with Japanese institutions, investors, and sustainability stakeholders. Summit Activities Include: Project presentations and pitching sessions Networking with industry leaders Engagement with Japanese institutions Study visits to enterprises in Japan Partnership-building opportunities Investment-focused discussions The summit represents a major opportunity for participants to showcase their green economy project ideas before international stakeholders. What Participants Will Learn By the end of the programme, participants will gain advanced expertise and practical skills in multiple areas of sustainable development and green finance. Key Learning Outcomes Include: 1. Green Finance and Investment Skills Participants will learn how to: Design bankable green economy projects Develop advanced financing structures Mobilize sustainable investment Create climate finance solutions 2. Climate Policy and Technical Expertise Participants will strengthen their understanding of: Climate policy frameworks Carbon markets Sustainable infrastructure Regional and global sustainability standards 3. Leadership and Regional Collaboration The programme also develops: Strategic leadership skills Cross-border collaboration capabilities Regional partnership-building expertise Japan-ASEAN cooperation strategies Programme Assignments and Expectations Participants are expected to actively engage throughout the programme. Assignments Include: Phase I Complete all online modules Submit an early-stage capstone project proposal Phase II Improve and expand the capstone project Participate in mentoring and peer-learning sessions Phase III Present and pitch the project during the Japan-ASEAN Green Investment Summit Who Can Apply? The programme welcomes applicants from ASEAN countries working in government, finance, sustainability, and related sectors. Eligible Applicants Include: Government Officials Professionals working in ministries or agencies related to: Finance Economy Energy Environment Trade Sustainable development Planning Private-Sector Professionals Applicants from industries such as: Banking Fintech Infrastructure Sustainability Energy Technology Investment Consulting Industry Project development Preferred Areas of Experience Applicants with experience or strong interest in the following fields are especially encouraged to apply: Energy transition Carbon markets Emission trading Sustainable banking Green fintech Green transformation policy Climate finance Public-private partnerships Eligibility Requirements To qualify, applicants must: Be from an ASEAN country Demonstrate interest or experience in sustainable development fields Be proficient in English Have reliable internet access Be computer literate Be willing to participate fully in programme activities Commit to completing all assignments and learning requirements Certificates and Recognition Participants who successfully complete Phase I will receive a: Certificate of Completion Participants advancing to later phases will receive additional certificates based on their participation. Financial Coverage The programme is offered: Completely Free of Charge Thanks to financial support from the Government and People of Japan. Selected Phase III participants traveling to Japan will benefit from international exposure and summit participation opportunities. Why This Programme Matters As ASEAN countries continue transitioning toward greener economies, there is growing demand for professionals capable of designing innovative sustainable finance and climate investment solutions. This programme provides a rare opportunity to: Gain practical green finance expertise Develop internationally relevant sustainability skills Collaborate with regional and Japanese experts Build investable green projects Expand professional networks across ASEAN and Japan Strengthen leadership in climate and sustainability sectors For professionals interested in climate finance, energy transition, carbon markets, and sustainable economic transformation, this programme offers a valuable platform for long-term career growth and regional impact. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 30 June 2026 (11:59 p.m. JST / UTC+9) Late submissions may not be accepted. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply online through the official UNITAR application portal. Apply Here Read More About UNITAR Established in 1965, the United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) is the training and capacity-building arm of the United Nations. UNITAR works globally to strengthen skills, institutional capacity, and leadership for sustainable development through innovative learning programmes, partnerships, and professional training initiatives aligned with the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. For more global Fellowship opportunities click here The post UNITAR Green Economic Acceleration Programme 2026 for ASEAN Professionals | Fully Funded Sustainable Green Finance Training in Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Future Network Weimar Triangle 2026: Fully Funded European Research Collaboration Opportunity for PhD Students and Postdoctoral Researchers
summary: The Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe programme is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers from Germany, France, and Poland to collaborate on innovative research projects addressing Europe’s most pressing challenges. Funded by the Federal Ministry of Research, Technology and Space and supported through the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD), this prestigious initiative seeks to strengthen trilateral scientific cooperation while empowering the next generation of European researchers. The programme creates a dynamic platform where PhD students and postdoctoral researchers can exchange ideas, build international research networks, and contribute to solutions shaping the future of Europe. About the Future Network Weimar Triangle Programme The Weimar Triangle was established in 1991 by Germany, France, and Poland to deepen cooperation and strengthen European unity. Since its creation, the partnership has served as a strategic framework for political, cultural, and scientific collaboration among the three countries. The Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe programme extends this vision into the research and innovation sector by bringing together outstanding young researchers from participating nations. The initiative focuses on interdisciplinary collaboration and aims to develop long-term partnerships capable of tackling critical global and European challenges through science, technology, and innovation. Participants will engage in trilateral research exchanges, academic cooperation, and international networking opportunities designed to foster impactful research outcomes and stronger European scientific resilience. Research Areas and Priority Themes Applicants are expected to conduct research aligned with one of the programme’s future-oriented thematic areas. 1. Climate-Neutral Energy and Climate Protection This area focuses on sustainable technologies and solutions that support Europe’s climate ambitions. Research topics may include: Fusion technologies Renewable energy expansion Energy storage systems Decarbonisation strategies Sustainable mobility Cross-border energy infrastructure Climate protection technologies 2. Artificial Intelligence, Digitalisation, and Microelectronics Researchers working in digital innovation and emerging technologies are strongly encouraged to apply. Possible research interests include: Artificial intelligence applications Ethical AI standards Cybersecurity Europe’s digital sovereignty Advanced microelectronics AI-enabled chip technologies Digital infrastructure innovation 3. Security in Europe and Research System Resilience The programme supports research that enhances Europe’s strategic preparedness and resilience. Relevant topics include: Knowledge security Research data protection Countering hybrid threats Risk management systems Critical raw materials security Strategic technologies Science communication and public trust 4. Biotechnology and Medicine of the Future This theme encourages innovative scientific work aimed at improving health systems, agriculture, and biotechnology solutions. Research areas may include: Sustainable health research Medical technologies Bioeconomy innovations Innovative therapies and diagnostics Food system resilience Agricultural biotechnology 5. Social Sciences and Humanities Perspectives on Technology The programme also recognizes the importance of understanding how technological transformations impact society. Potential topics include: Human-machine interaction Societal implications of new technologies Governance and democracy in the digital era Ethical technology development Social adaptation to emerging technologies Benefits of the Future Network Weimar Triangle Programme Selected participants will benefit from numerous academic and professional opportunities, including: International Research Collaboration Participants will work closely with researchers and institutions from Germany, France, and Poland, strengthening international academic cooperation. Expanded Professional Networks The programme enables researchers to build strong European networks that may support future research partnerships and career opportunities. Exposure to High-Level Research Environments Applicants gain access to leading institutions, experts, and innovative scientific communities across Europe. Contribution to European Innovation Researchers will directly contribute to solving major European and global challenges through interdisciplinary scientific collaboration. Career Development Opportunities The programme supports early-career researchers in enhancing their academic visibility, research experience, and international competitiveness. Why This Programme Matters Europe is currently facing major transitions in technology, climate policy, healthcare, energy systems, and security. The Future Network Weimar Triangle programme seeks to ensure that young researchers are actively involved in shaping solutions for these challenges. By encouraging cooperation among France, Germany, and Poland, the programme strengthens scientific diplomacy and promotes long-term European resilience through research and innovation. The initiative also aligns with the objectives of Germany’s High-Tech Agenda and the coalition agreement “Responsibility for Germany,” especially in areas related to education, research, and technological advancement. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to: PhD students from France and Poland Exceptionally qualified postdoctoral researchers from France and Poland Researchers connected to participating institutions in Germany, France, and Poland Applicants should demonstrate strong academic potential and research interests connected to the programme’s thematic priorities. Application Process Interested applicants are encouraged to apply through the official DAAD Future Network Weimar Triangle platform. Applicants may also participate in the programme survey to receive updates and additional information regarding application procedures, programme activities, and future announcements. Deadline No official application deadline was specified in the provided information. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible and monitor the official programme page for updates. APPLY HERE Final Thoughts The Future Network Weimar Triangle: Science for Europe programme represents an outstanding opportunity for ambitious researchers seeking international collaboration, interdisciplinary engagement, and impactful scientific work. For PhD students and postdoctoral researchers passionate about solving Europe’s future challenges, this programme offers a valuable platform to contribute to meaningful innovation while building lasting international academic partnerships. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Future Network Weimar Triangle 2026: Fully Funded European Research Collaboration Opportunity for PhD Students and Postdoctoral Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded PhD Positions at Penn State University (Fall 2026): Research in Structural Engineering, Advanced Materials, Automated Construction, and AI-Assisted Simulation
summary: The Pennsylvania State University (Penn State) is inviting applications from highly motivated and research-driven students for fully funded PhD positions beginning in Fall 2026 at its University Park campus. These doctoral opportunities will be supervised by Dr. Botong Zheng, who holds appointments in both the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering and the Department of Architectural Engineering. The positions are expected to be fully funded and will provide competitive graduate assistantship packages for successful candidates. This opportunity is designed for students who are passionate about conducting cutting-edge research at the intersection of structural engineering, advanced materials, automated construction technologies, computational modeling, large-scale experimental testing, and artificial intelligence-assisted simulation. About the Research Group Dr. Botong Zheng s research group focuses on solving complex engineering challenges by integrating experimental research, computational techniques, advanced materials, and intelligent technologies. The group aims to pursue pioneering and impactful research rather than incremental studies focused solely on publication output. Students joining the group will have the opportunity to work on innovative projects that advance the future of infrastructure, construction technologies, materials engineering, and structural systems. Research activities may span multiple disciplines and involve collaborations with researchers, institutions, and federally funded programs across the United States and internationally. What the Fully Funded PhD Position Offers Successful applicants can expect access to numerous academic and research benefits, including: Fully Funded Doctoral Studies The selected PhD students will receive: Full financial support for doctoral studies. Competitive graduate assistantship packages. Funding support associated with research projects. Access to Penn State s world-class research facilities and laboratories. Opportunities to collaborate with leading researchers and industry partners. High-Impact Research Environment Students will engage in research that combines: Structural engineering. Mechanics and material behavior. Advanced construction technologies. Artificial intelligence applications. Computational simulation. Infrastructure resilience and performance evaluation. The research environment is designed to help students build deep technical expertise while addressing real-world engineering challenges. Multidisciplinary Research Opportunities Depending on project alignment and funding availability, students may participate in: Federally funded research initiatives. Multi-institutional collaborations. Interdisciplinary engineering projects. Large-scale infrastructure testing programs. Advanced manufacturing and automated construction studies. Research Areas and Technical Focus The research group is particularly interested in students who possess strong preparation in one or both of the following areas. Experimental, Fabrication, and Hands-On Research Students interested in experimental research will contribute to projects involving advanced materials, automated construction systems, and infrastructure-scale testing. Relevant experience may include: Structural laboratory testing. Experimental design and setup. Instrumentation systems. Data acquisition systems. Automated fabrication technologies. Material development and characterization. Infrastructure testing and validation. Building and bridge engineering experiments. Aerospace structural testing. Manufacturing process development. Candidates with practical laboratory experience and strong problem-solving abilities will be particularly competitive. Computational Modeling, Programming, and AI-Assisted Simulation Students interested in computational research will work on advanced modeling and simulation techniques related to materials, structures, and construction systems. Relevant expertise may include: Programming and software development. Finite element modeling. Computational mechanics. Numerical simulation. Artificial intelligence and machine learning applications. Digital twin technologies. Infrastructure performance prediction. Material behavior simulation. Construction process modeling. Data-driven engineering methods. Applicants with strong coding skills and experience using computational tools will be highly valued. Research Topics Students May Explore Potential research directions may include: Advanced structural materials. Automated construction and robotic fabrication. Large-scale structural testing. Smart infrastructure systems. AI-assisted engineering simulations. Computational mechanics. Infrastructure resilience under extreme environments. Material microstructure modeling. Long-term infrastructure performance. Data-driven structural engineering. Sustainable construction technologies. Intelligent monitoring systems. Expectations for PhD Students The research group seeks students who are committed to developing into independent researchers and future leaders in academia, industry, or government research organizations. All PhD students are expected to develop strong capabilities in: Research Analysis Students should be capable of: Designing rigorous research methodologies. Analyzing complex datasets. Drawing evidence-based conclusions. Testing and validating hypotheses. Technical Communication Students must develop skills in: Scientific writing. Journal paper preparation. Conference presentations. Technical reporting. Research dissemination. Independent Research Candidates should be prepared to: Learn new technical skills quickly. Work independently. Solve open-ended research problems. Persist through uncertainty and technical challenges. Contribute original ideas to research projects. The group emphasizes intellectual curiosity, technical excellence, and long-term impact rather than simply accumulating credentials or publication counts. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for students who: Want to pursue a research-intensive PhD. Aspire to become experts in structural engineering and advanced technologies. Enjoy solving challenging engineering problems. Have strong analytical and technical skills. Are interested in both theory and practical implementation. Seek to contribute to innovations that can transform infrastructure and construction industries. The position is especially suitable for applicants interested in bridging the gap between experimental engineering, computational science, and artificial intelligence. Application Process Because the regular Fall 2026 graduate admission deadline has already passed, interested applicants must first complete the research group screening process. Candidates should submit materials through the research group screening form before being considered for the next stage. Required Application Materials Applicants must provide: Completed Screening Form All questions in the group screening form must be completed accurately and thoroughly. Curriculum Vitae (CV) Submit a PDF-only file named: LastName_FirstName_CV.pdf The CV should include: Educational background. Research experience. Technical skills. Publications (if applicable). Awards and honors. Professional achievements. Relevant academic activities. Statement of Research Interests Submit a PDF-only file named: LastName_FirstName_SRI.pdf This document is considered one of the most important parts of the application. The statement should be no longer than two pages and clearly explain: Your research interests. Your preferred research direction. Relevant technical skills. Previous research experiences. Academic strengths. Career goals. Why you are interested in joining Dr. Zheng s research group. How your background aligns with the group s research focus. Applicants are encouraged to provide specific examples that demonstrate their readiness for advanced doctoral research. Why Penn State University Park? Penn State s University Park campus is one of the leading research universities in the United States, offering outstanding facilities, interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities, and a vibrant academic environment. Students joining this research group will gain access to: State-of-the-art laboratories. Leading faculty mentorship. Large-scale research infrastructure. National and international collaborations. High-impact research opportunities. Strong professional development pathways. For students seeking a challenging and rewarding doctoral experience in structural engineering, advanced materials, automated construction, and AI-assisted simulation, this fully funded PhD opportunity represents an excellent pathway toward a successful research career. Application Deadline No specific application deadline has been announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible through the group screening process due to the competitive nature of the opportunity. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Fully Funded PhD Positions at Penn State University. Explore more fully funded PhD opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Fully Funded PhD Positions at Penn State University (Fall 2026): Research in Structural Engineering, Advanced Materials, Automated Construction, and AI-Assisted Simulation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship 2026 at the Hamilton Institute
summary: Applications are now open for the Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship 2026 at the Hamilton Institute. This fully funded doctoral scholarship is designed for highly qualified candidates interested in cognitive computational neuroscience, decision-making, neural network modelling, and human brain research. The scholarship offers the chance to work under expert supervision in a world-class research environment and develop advanced skills in computational modelling, empirical research, and scientific publication. Scholarship Overview The Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship is a four-year, fully funded program commencing in September or October 2026. It is attached to the Hamilton Institute and supervised by Dr Peter Murphy from the Department of Psychology. Project Title: Identifying the Neurocomputational Basis for Making Decisions Abstracted from Sensation and Action The scholarship supports a PhD candidate to investigate how the human brain forms abstract decisions independent of specific sensory input or motor actions. This work combines cognitive neuroscience, psychology, artificial intelligence, and computational modelling. Benefits of the Scholarship The Maynooth University Doctoral Scholarship provides: Annual student stipend of €25,000 Full tuition fee support for four years Research training through Maynooth University’s Graduate Research Academy Opportunities to present research at local, national, and international conferences Support in preparing peer-reviewed journal publications Hands-on experience in neural network modelling, EEG studies, and computational neuroscience About the Research Project The project focuses on understanding the brain signal that reflects supramodal accumulation of information over time. This process allows humans to make decisions abstracted from immediate sensory or motor cues. The PhD research will follow three major directions: Trained Neural Networks – Identify conditions under which abstract information accumulation emerges. Biologically Constrained Network Modelling – Investigate plausible neural implementations through computational models informed by biology. Empirical Testing in Humans – Validate model predictions using behavioural and EEG measurements of abstract decision signals. Responsibilities of the PhD Student The successful candidate will: Conduct computational research using neural network models Design and analyse EEG studies and behavioural experiments Collect, manage, and analyse research data according to FAIR principles Present findings at conferences and local meetings Prepare draft publications for peer-reviewed journals Undertake relevant training and engage with research development programs Work independently and collaboratively under supervision Mode of Study Full-time residency at Maynooth University in Ireland for four years Research primarily based at Maynooth University campus Research outside campus requires supervisor and Head of Department permission Subject to Maynooth University Doctoral Scholarship terms and Research Student Programme regulations Eligibility Criteria Essential Requirements: First class or upper second class 2.1 honours undergraduate degree in Neuroscience, Cognitive Science, Computer Science, Psychology, or related discipline Independent research experience with deep learning, neural networks, behavioural data, or EEG studies Proficiency in quantitative analysis Excellent written and oral communication in English Desirable Qualifications: Master’s degree in a relevant field with a significant research component Computational skills (Python, MATLAB) Familiarity with neural network platforms (TensorFlow, PyTorch) Experience with GPU or cluster-based computing Experience with human psychophysics and scalp EEG studies Application Process Applicants must submit: Personal statement (max 600 words) highlighting motivation and experience Curriculum Vitae including education, research experience, quantitative and computational skills, publications Relevant academic transcripts Names and contact details of two academic referees Submission: Email applications to Dr Peter Murphy at Peter.Murphy@mu.ie with the subject line: Doctoral Scholarship – Hamilton Institute/Psychology 2026 Deadline: 5pm on 1 July 2026 Note: Non-native English speakers must provide proof of English proficiency . Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview. Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Fully Funded Maynooth University Faculty of Science and Engineering Doctoral Scholarship 2026 at the Hamilton Institute appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNESCO Media and Information Literacy Alliance Launches First Call for Chapter Creation 2026: Global Opportunity to Build Regional MIL Networks and Strengthen Media Literacy Worldwide
summary: The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) Media and Information Literacy (MIL) Alliance has officially launched its first global call for the creation of MIL Alliance Chapters. This landmark initiative marks a major step toward strengthening collaboration, inclusivity, and localized action in media and information literacy across the world. The MIL Alliance is designed as a “network of networks,” bringing together experts, institutions, and organizations committed to advancing media and information literacy. With this new chapter system, UNESCO is expanding opportunities for members to organize into strategic sub-groups based on shared interests, regions, or thematic priorities. These chapters will enable more effective, context-specific interventions to address misinformation, improve digital literacy, and strengthen responsible communication globally. What is the UNESCO MIL Alliance Chapter Initiative? The MIL Alliance Chapter system is a structural innovation introduced by UNESCO to improve how its global members collaborate and implement media literacy initiatives. Key Purpose of the Chapters The chapters are designed to: Strengthen regional and thematic collaboration among MIL Alliance members Promote localized solutions to media and information literacy challenges Overcome linguistic and cultural barriers in information access Support advocacy for institutionalizing MIL policies with governments Increase outreach to underrepresented communities Foster collective ownership of MIL initiatives worldwide These chapters represent an operational expansion of the MIL Alliance, making its global mission more practical, decentralized, and inclusive. Why This Initiative Matters Globally Media and information literacy has become essential in an era dominated by digital content, misinformation, and rapid technological change. UNESCO’s initiative directly addresses these challenges by empowering local and regional actors. Major global impacts include: Improved ability to identify misinformation and fake news Stronger education systems integrating media literacy Empowerment of youth as critical thinkers and responsible communicators Enhanced policy advocacy at national and regional levels Better collaboration across continents and cultural contexts This initiative is particularly significant for regions like Africa, Latin America, the Caribbean, and other underserved areas where access to structured media literacy programs is still developing. Structure and Governance of MIL Alliance Chapters The MIL Alliance operates under a structured governance system to ensure transparency and accountability. Key Governance Features Chapters are reviewed and validated by the Global Board The Global Board provides strategic oversight and coordination UNESCO works closely with the Board in guiding implementation Chapters must meet eligibility criteria and submit structured proposals Reporting and development of chapters are continuously monitored The Global Board was recently established through a global election process and now serves as the main decision-making body for the Alliance. Who Can Apply for MIL Alliance Chapter Creation? Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be members of the UNESCO MIL Alliance Represent organizations, institutions, or expert groups involved in media and information literacy Demonstrate capacity to coordinate or contribute to MIL-related initiatives Show alignment with UNESCO’s values of inclusion, education, and information access Have interest in regional or thematic collaboration Geographic Eligibility This opportunity is open globally , including: Africa Europe Asia Latin America and the Caribbean North America Oceania Other Eligibility Notes Open to all races and ethnic backgrounds Applicants must be part of the MIL Alliance network (currently spanning 117 countries) Both institutional and expert-led groups may apply depending on guidelines Application Deadline The first call for MIL Alliance Chapter creation is open until: 31 July 2026 After this period, calls will be issued every two years for a duration of two months. Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive and global nature of the review process. Application Process Overview Interested MIL Alliance members are encouraged to: Review official UNESCO MIL Alliance chapter guidelines Identify thematic or regional focus areas Prepare a structured chapter proposal Submit application through the official UNESCO MIL Alliance platform Await review and validation by the Global Board Key Benefits of Joining or Creating a Chapter Opportunity to influence global media literacy policy Access to UNESCO’s international MIL network Increased visibility for regional initiatives Collaboration with experts across 117 countries Capacity building in media literacy education Contribution to global efforts against misinformation Strengthened institutional partnerships APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post UNESCO Media and Information Literacy Alliance Launches First Call for Chapter Creation 2026: Global Opportunity to Build Regional MIL Networks and Strengthen Media Literacy Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Doctoral Researcher (f/m/diverse) at IDOS Germany 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Food Systems, Sustainability, and Behavioral Research
summary: The German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) has officially opened applications for an exciting Doctoral Researcher (f/m/diverse) opportunity under the internationally recognized FoodDiverse research initiative. This position presents a remarkable opportunity for aspiring researchers and future academics who want to combine professional research experience with a fully supported PhD journey focused on sustainable food systems, nutrition, behavioral transformation, and development policy. The doctoral position is hosted within the DFG-funded Collaborative Research Center (CRC 1780) “Food System Diversification for Sustainable Nutrition” (FoodDiverse), a major interdisciplinary research initiative jointly implemented by the University of Bonn, IDOS, and the University of Ghana. Selected candidates will become part of a globally connected research environment examining how diversified food systems can improve nutrition, sustainability, and planetary health across Germany and Ghana. The position is expected to begin on 01 October 2026 and will initially run for three years, with the possibility of an extension of up to nine additional months. About the German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) The German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) is one of Germany’s leading research institutions focused on development policy and sustainability studies. The institute is widely recognized for producing high-quality research, offering independent policy advice, and promoting international cooperation around sustainable futures. IDOS combines academic excellence with practical policy engagement, creating a research environment where doctoral researchers can contribute directly to global conversations on sustainability, development, social transformation, and international cooperation. Researchers at IDOS benefit from: Internationally recognized mentorship Participation in research workshops and conferences Exposure to global policy discussions Opportunities for international collaboration Research stays abroad Interdisciplinary teamwork Structured doctoral supervision Overview of the FoodDiverse Research Project The selected doctoral researcher will join the FoodDiverse Collaborative Research Center, a major research consortium focused on understanding the relationship between food system diversification and sustainable nutrition. The project brings together experts from various disciplines, including: Agronomy Sociology Agricultural Economics Environmental Sciences Nutrition Human Health Ecology Political Sciences Anthropology Researchers within the project investigate how agricultural diversity, market structures, and dietary patterns influence both human health and environmental sustainability. The doctoral researcher will specifically contribute to the subproject titled: “Behavioral Strategies to Diversify Food Consumption” The research will focus heavily on: Plant-based protein consumption Food behavior transformation Policy and discourse analysis Sustainable nutrition systems Social and cultural food practices Consumer behavior in Germany and Ghana Research Focus and PhD Structure The doctoral project is embedded within the IDOS Dual PhD Programme, which combines advanced academic research with practical policy-oriented engagement. The PhD research will involve empirical fieldwork in both Germany and Ghana, allowing the researcher to study food consumption patterns and behavioral transformation across different social and cultural contexts. A major focus of the research includes: Investigating plant-based protein intake across social groups Exploring discourse around sustainable food systems Analyzing policy frameworks influencing food diversification Studying transformational consumption behavior Examining sustainable nutrition strategies The doctoral candidate will be supervised by: Prof. Dr. Anna-Katharina Hornidge Jun.-Prof. Dr. Dominic Lemken Prof. Dr. Monika Hartmann The University of Bonn Faculty of Social Sciences will serve as the degree-awarding institution. What Makes This PhD Opportunity Unique? This opportunity goes beyond a standard doctoral position. It combines: 1. Academic Research Excellence Researchers receive direct access to: International conferences Research workshops High-level scientific mentoring Collaborative interdisciplinary networks 2. Structured PhD Support IDOS offers a semi-structured PhD programme including: Group mentoring Peer mentoring One-on-one supervision Research training activities Dissertation support At least 50% of the working time is dedicated exclusively to dissertation research. 3. International Fieldwork Experience The project includes fieldwork in: Ghana Germany This provides practical international research exposure and cross-cultural research experience. 4. Policy and Real-World Impact The research contributes directly to ongoing discussions on: Sustainable nutrition Food security Climate-sensitive diets Development policy Behavioral transformation Job Responsibilities The selected doctoral researcher will undertake several important responsibilities within the FoodDiverse project. Main Tasks Include: Developing an individual PhD research agenda Designing conceptual and methodological research frameworks Conducting empirical fieldwork in Ghana and Germany Performing discourse and policy analysis Collaborating with interdisciplinary research teams Working closely with project partners in both countries Participating actively in the IDOS Dual PhD Programme Contributing to institutional activities at IDOS Eligibility Criteria and Qualifications Applicants must demonstrate strong academic and research potential. Required Qualifications Candidates should possess: A Master’s degree in relevant disciplines such as: Sociology Human Geography Political Science Anthropology Agricultural Sciences Nutrition Sciences Required Skills and Experience Applicants should also demonstrate: Knowledge of qualitative social science research methods Experience with ethnographic research Strong communication abilities Excellent academic writing skills Organizational competence Motivation to pursue doctoral research Ability to work in interdisciplinary teams Additional Advantages The following are considered strong assets: Research or professional experience in Africa Knowledge of German language International collaboration experience Language Requirements Applicants must have: Fluent written and spoken English German language proficiency is beneficial but not mandatory. Travel Requirement Candidates must be willing to conduct international travel and fieldwork, including work in tropical climates. Salary and Employment Benefits The doctoral researcher position offers competitive compensation under the German public sector salary framework. Salary Information Salary Scale: EG 13 TVoeD Bund Gross Salary Range: €40,000 – €58,000 annually (65% employment basis) Working Hours Part-time position 65% employment 25.4 working hours per week Additional Employee Benefits IDOS provides numerous professional and social benefits, including: Work Flexibility Flexible working hours Hybrid work options Home office possibilities within Germany (up to 60%) Financial Benefits Annual special payment Employer pension contribution Subsidized Deutschland Job Ticket Leave and Vacation Up to 30 vacation days annually Additional company vacation days Professional Development Training opportunities Career development support International academic exposure Diversity and Inclusion Commitment IDOS actively promotes equality, diversity, and inclusion in its recruitment process. Applications are strongly encouraged from: Women People of colour Persons with disabilities Individuals from all nationalities Applicants with migration backgrounds Diverse gender identities and sexual orientations The institute is certified under the “workandfamily” audit program, demonstrating its commitment to work-life balance and inclusive employment practices. Required Application Documents Applicants must prepare the following documents: Cover Letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) Academic Certificates Supporting documentation Candidates with foreign university degrees must additionally provide proof of degree recognition through the Anabin database excerpt recognized by the German Central Office for Foreign Education (ZAB). Application Deadline Applications must be submitted no later than: 14 June 2026 at 23:59 CET Late applications may not be considered. Interview Timeline Shortlisted candidates are expected to participate in interviews scheduled for: July 2026 How to Apply Applications must be submitted exclusively through the official IDOS application portal. Applicants should reference: Position Number: 2026-B-02 Click here for more and to apply Why You Should Apply This doctoral researcher opportunity at IDOS offers much more than a traditional PhD position. It is a chance to: Conduct internationally relevant sustainability research Work alongside globally respected researchers Gain interdisciplinary and international research experience Influence policy discussions on sustainable nutrition Develop advanced qualitative research expertise Build a strong international academic network Earn a competitive salary while pursuing a PhD For aspiring researchers passionate about food systems, sustainability, behavioral transformation, and global development, this opportunity represents a major step toward an impactful international academic career. For more global job opportunities click here The post Doctoral Researcher (f/m/diverse) at IDOS Germany 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Food Systems, Sustainability, and Behavioral Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: BRICS STI Framework Programme Opens Pilot STI Flagship Projects Call 2026 for International Research Funding Collaboration
summary: The BRICS STI Framework Programme has officially announced the opening of the Pilot STI Flagship Projects Call 2026 , a major international funding opportunity designed to strengthen scientific collaboration, technological innovation, and research partnerships across BRICS member countries and partner nations. This call provides a platform for researchers, institutions, and innovation-driven organizations to jointly develop high-impact scientific and technological projects that address global and regional challenges. The initiative promotes cross-border cooperation in science, technology, and innovation (STI), reinforcing shared development goals among participating countries. Overview of the Pilot STI Flagship Projects Call 2026 The Pilot STI Flagship Projects Call 2026 is a competitive international funding initiative under the BRICS STI cooperation framework. It supports collaborative research and innovation projects that involve multiple participating countries. The call is part of a broader effort to enhance: Scientific research cooperation Technology transfer and innovation Joint problem-solving in global challenges Capacity building among BRICS and partner research institutions Key Dates and Deadline Application Opening: Ongoing Submission Deadline: June 16, 2026 at 15:00 (UTC+3, Moscow Time) Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline to avoid technical delays or last-minute submission issues. Participating Countries This funding call is open to collaborative projects involving researchers and institutions from the following countries: Brazil China Egypt Iran Russia South Africa These countries represent a strong global network of emerging economies committed to advancing science and technology collaboration. Funding and Collaboration Scope The program supports flagship-level research projects , meaning proposals should demonstrate: High scientific impact and innovation potential Strong international collaboration across at least two participating countries Clear relevance to global or regional challenges Feasibility and sustainability of research outcomes Projects may span various scientific and technological fields, including but not limited to: Engineering and applied sciences Health and biomedical research Artificial intelligence and digital transformation Environmental sustainability and climate research Energy systems and renewable technologies Agricultural innovation and food security Application Requirements Applicants are required to submit a Joint Application Form (JAF) developed under the BRICS STI Framework Programme. Supporting documents include: Official Call Announcement Joint Application Form (JAF) National Annexes from participating funding agencies Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ document) Each participating country has its own national funding annex and guidelines that must be followed during submission. National Funding Agencies Involved The following national agencies coordinate participation in this program: National Council for Scientific and Technological Development (CNPq) – Brazil Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST) – China Science, Technology Innovation Funding Authority (STDF) – Egypt Iran National Science Foundation (INSF) – Iran Ministry of Science and Higher Education (MSHE) – Russia National Research Foundation (NRF) – South Africa These agencies play a key role in evaluating, funding, and supporting approved research projects within their respective countries. Key Objectives of the Program The Pilot STI Flagship Projects Call 2026 aims to: Strengthen multilateral scientific cooperation among BRICS countries Promote cutting-edge research and technological innovation Support sustainable development through science-based solutions Enhance mobility and collaboration among researchers Foster long-term international research networks Expected Impact of Selected Projects Successful projects are expected to: Deliver innovative scientific outputs with global relevance Improve research capacity across participating institutions Generate policy-relevant scientific findings Promote technology transfer and commercialization opportunities Strengthen BRICS STI cooperation framework How to Apply Applicants must: Form a consortium involving eligible institutions from participating countries Complete the Joint Application Form (JAF) Review and comply with national annex requirements Submit applications through the official BRICS STI submission platform Ensure compliance with the deadline: June 16, 2026, 15:00 UTC+3 Important Documents Call Announcement (PDF) Joint Application Form (DOCX) National Annexes (country-specific guidelines) Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ PDF) Final Note The Pilot STI Flagship Projects Call 2026 represents a significant opportunity for international research collaboration within the BRICS STI Framework Programme. It is designed to support transformative scientific projects that can contribute to sustainable development and global innovation leadership. Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all national and program guidelines before submission. CLICK HERE TO APPLY For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here . title: Zendesk Foundation Tech for Good Impact Awards 2026: Complete Guide to Funding, Free Software, and Global Nonprofit Support (Up to $50,000 Grant Opportunity)
summary: The Zendesk Foundation Tech for Good Impact Awards 2026 is a global funding and technology support program designed to empower nonprofit organizations that are actively solving some of the world’s most pressing social challenges. Through a combination of cash grants, free enterprise software access, and pro bono technical support , Zendesk is enabling nonprofits to scale their impact, improve service delivery, and strengthen community outcomes. Each year, selected organizations receive access to Zendesk’s AI-powered customer service platform along with financial support ranging from $5,000 to $50,000 . The 2026 application cycle is now open, offering nonprofits a powerful opportunity to enhance operations and expand their reach. About the Zendesk Tech for Good Impact Awards The Tech for Good Impact Awards is an initiative under the Zendesk Foundation that focuses on leveraging technology to improve social outcomes. It is part of Zendesk’s broader mission of “greater impact, greater good,” where technology is used to support vulnerable communities and nonprofit organizations globally. The program provides: Free access to Zendesk’s customer service and support software Cash grants between $5,000 and $50,000 Pro bono technical and implementation support Tools powered by AI to improve service delivery efficiency Core Areas of Focus Eligible nonprofit organizations must work within one or more of the following impact areas: 1. Community Building Organizations that: Strengthen social connections Improve access to essential resources Support community resilience and inclusion 2. Crisis Response and Humanitarian Support Programs that: Respond to disasters and emergencies Provide rapid information and relief services Reduce human suffering during crises 3. Career Pathways into Technology Initiatives that: Train underrepresented groups for tech careers Support workforce development Bridge the digital and economic opportunity gap Funding and Support Details Successful applicants may receive: Cash grants ranging from $5,000 to $50,000 Full access to Zendesk’s customer service platform AI-powered tools to automate and improve support workflows Technical assistance and implementation support Capacity-building resources for scaling nonprofit impact This combined package is designed to help nonprofits not only fund their projects but also modernize their operations. Application Deadline Application Deadline: July 15, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET Applications submitted after this date will not be considered. Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Zendesk Tech for Good Impact Awards 2026, applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be a registered nonprofit organization Must provide proof of nonprofit status during application Must submit applications via the official Zendesk application form only Must complete the application in English Must not be a current Zendesk customer or past Zendesk grantee Must operate within eligible impact areas (community building, crisis response, or tech career pathways) Must not be located in restricted regions (including countries under international restrictions such as North Korea, Syria, Iran, and others as specified by Zendesk guidelines) Application Process Overview Applicants are required to: Complete the online application form Provide organizational details and mission statement Describe the population served and impact goals Explain how Zendesk technology will be used to improve service delivery Submit required legal documentation (proof of nonprofit status) Agree to terms and conditions Why This Program Matters The Zendesk Tech for Good Impact Awards plays a significant role in helping nonprofits: Scale operations without high software costs Improve communication with beneficiaries Automate support services using AI tools Respond faster during emergencies Create sustainable pathways into tech employment APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here . title: Africa Extractives Media Fellowship 2026 for Ghanaian Journalists | Applications Open for Cohort II
summary: Applications are now officially open for the second cohort of the Africa Extractives Media Fellowship (AEMF), an intensive six-month journalism capacity-building programme designed to strengthen extractives-sector reporting in Africa. Organized by NewsWire Africa, the fellowship is aimed at empowering Ghanaian journalists with advanced reporting skills, investigative techniques, data journalism expertise, and in-depth knowledge of Africa’s extractive industries. The fellowship addresses critical reporting gaps across sectors such as mining, oil and gas, climate transition, critical minerals, environmental governance, and revenue accountability. Aspiring and practicing journalists in Ghana who are passionate about impactful storytelling, public accountability, and investigative reporting are encouraged to apply for this prestigious programme. About the Africa Extractives Media Fellowship (AEMF) The Africa Extractives Media Fellowship is a specialized journalism programme established by NewsWire Africa to improve the quality, depth, and impact of extractives-sector reporting across the African continent. Africa’s extractive industries continue to shape economies, governance systems, environmental policies, and community livelihoods. However, reporting on these sectors often lacks technical depth, investigative rigor, and sustained public-interest focus. The AEMF was created to bridge this gap by equipping journalists with: Specialized extractives-sector knowledge Data-driven reporting skills Investigative journalism techniques Ethical reporting practices Understanding of climate and environmental issues Policy and governance reporting expertise The programme combines practical learning, mentorship, industry engagement, and hands-on reporting experiences to strengthen journalism that drives accountability and informed public discourse. Programme Duration and Structure The fellowship is a: Six-Month In-Person Journalism Fellowship Participants selected for Cohort II will undergo intensive training, mentorship, reporting assignments, and collaborative learning activities focused on Africa’s extractive sectors. The programme creates opportunities for fellows to engage directly with: Industry leaders Policymakers Civil society organizations Academic experts Environmental advocates Governance institutions Key Areas of Focus The fellowship covers several important themes within the extractives industry. Major Focus Areas Include: 1. Mining, Oil and Gas, and Green Minerals Participants will gain deeper understanding of: Resource extraction systems Energy industries Critical minerals Green transition resources Mining sector developments 2. Revenue Flows and Governance Fellows will explore: Resource revenue management Public accountability Transparency systems Governance structures Economic policy implications 3. Environmental Justice and Community Impact The programme also focuses on: Environmental protection Community displacement Ecological sustainability Social justice Local development challenges 4. Climate Transition and Critical Minerals Participants will examine: Africa’s role in global energy transition Critical minerals demand Climate adaptation Sustainable resource governance 5. Ethical, Data-Driven, and Investigative Journalism The fellowship strengthens participants’ abilities in: Investigative storytelling Fact-checking Data analysis Ethical journalism practices Evidence-based reporting Why the Fellowship Matters Africa’s extractive industries generate billions in revenue and influence major development decisions, yet many critical stories remain underreported. This fellowship helps journalists build the expertise needed to investigate and explain complex issues such as: Mineral revenue distribution Corporate accountability Environmental degradation Illegal mining activities Climate-related resource transitions Community rights Public policy failures Investment and governance systems By strengthening journalism in these areas, the fellowship contributes to greater transparency, accountability, and informed public engagement. Fellowship Impact So Far The inaugural Ghana Cohort (2025–2026) already demonstrated significant impact through high-quality reporting produced by fellows. Published Impact Stories Included: Coverage of Ghana’s sliding-scale royalty framework Reports on investor concerns and industry responses Historical mineral revenue analysis Governance and accountability investigations These stories were featured by major Ghanaian media outlets including: Citi Newsroom MyJoyOnline GhanaWeb The High Street Journal Fellowship Partners and Institutional Support The programme is supported by a strong network of national and international institutions, including: Australian High Commission Ghana Chamber of Mines University of Ghana Business School University of Mines and Technology Petroleum Hub Development Corporation CDD-Ghana Digital Earth Africa WANEP National Petroleum Authority Public Interest Accountability Committee (PIAC) Tema Oil Refinery (TOR) Ltd. Ayatickets The inaugural cohort was launched at the residence of the Australian High Commissioner with participation from government officials, diplomats, industry leaders, academia, and civil society stakeholders. Benefits of Joining the Fellowship Selected fellows will benefit from a wide range of professional development opportunities. Fellows Will Gain: Advanced investigative journalism skills Specialized extractives-sector knowledge Data journalism experience Networking opportunities Mentorship from experts Exposure to policy and governance discussions Access to industry stakeholders Portfolio-building opportunities Increased professional visibility The fellowship also offers participants opportunities to produce impactful stories capable of shaping national conversations and influencing policy debates. Who Can Apply? The programme is primarily designed for: Ghanaian Journalists This includes journalists working in: Print media Online journalism Television Radio Multimedia storytelling Investigative reporting Applicants interested in: Extractives reporting Environmental journalism Climate reporting Governance journalism Business and economic reporting are strongly encouraged to apply. Ideal Candidate Profile Strong applicants are likely to demonstrate: Passion for public-interest journalism Interest in accountability reporting Curiosity about extractive industries Commitment to ethical journalism Willingness to participate fully in the six-month programme Strong storytelling ability Interest in climate and sustainability issues Application Deadline Applications for Cohort II close on: Monday, 8 June 2026 at 23:59 GMT Applicants are encouraged to submit early before the deadline. How to Apply Interested applicants should apply through the official AEMF Fellowship Portal. Click Here for more and to apply Why Journalists Should Apply The Africa Extractives Media Fellowship offers a rare opportunity for Ghanaian journalists to gain highly specialized reporting expertise in one of Africa’s most important sectors. The programme helps journalists: Strengthen investigative reporting skills Build expertise in extractives governance Produce impactful public-interest stories Connect with industry experts and policymakers Improve data and environmental reporting capacity Contribute to accountability and transparency in Africa’s resource sectors For journalists passionate about uncovering impactful stories and shaping public conversations around natural resources, governance, and sustainability, the AEMF Fellowship represents a valuable career-building opportunity. Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post Africa Extractives Media Fellowship 2026 for Ghanaian Journalists | Applications Open for Cohort II appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: COP31 Climate Change Media Partnership Fellowship 2026 in Türkiye for Journalists from Developing Countries
summary: Applications are now officially open for the prestigious COP31 Climate Change Media Partnership (CCMP) Reporting Fellowship 2026, an internationally recognized opportunity supporting journalists from low- and middle-income countries to report directly from the United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP31) in Antalya, Türkiye. The fellowship is organized by Internews’ Earth Journalism Network (EJN) in partnership with the Stanley Center for Peace and Security. The program aims to strengthen climate journalism worldwide by equipping reporters with direct access to global climate negotiations, high-level experts, editorial mentorship, and international reporting opportunities. The 31st United Nations Climate Change Conference of the Parties (COP31) will take place from November 9 to 20, 2026, in Antalya, Türkiye, and is expected to become one of the most significant climate policy gatherings in recent years. This year’s summit has already been described as the “Implementation COP,” focusing on how countries move from climate promises and commitments toward real implementation, financing, accountability, and measurable climate action. About the COP31 Climate Change Media Partnership Fellowship The Climate Change Media Partnership (CCMP) Fellowship is one of the world’s leading climate journalism support programs. Since its launch in 2007, the initiative has supported more than 550 journalists from developing countries to attend and report from annual United Nations climate negotiations. The fellowship enables journalists to: Report directly from UN climate negotiations Access exclusive interviews with global leaders and experts Receive mentorship from experienced climate journalists Strengthen climate reporting skills Build international journalism networks Deliver impactful climate stories to local audiences The 2026 edition introduces an important expansion by welcoming creator journalists for the first time. Independent content creators who use journalistic practices and digital storytelling to produce climate-related content for online audiences are now eligible through a separate creator journalist application track. Why COP31 Matters COP31 is expected to be one of the most influential climate summits in recent years as governments worldwide face mounting pressure to implement climate commitments. Key themes likely to dominate discussions include: Climate finance Renewable energy transitions Fossil fuel reduction Adaptation and resilience Loss and damage financing Food systems and sustainability Climate justice Carbon reduction implementation Global emissions accountability Journalists selected for the fellowship will have direct access to these negotiations and the opportunity to explain their implications to audiences back home. What the Fellowship Covers The COP31 CCMP Fellowship is fully funded and designed to remove financial barriers for journalists from developing regions. Selected fellows will receive comprehensive support including: 1. International Travel Support The fellowship covers: Round-trip economy-class airfare Ground transportation Travel medical insurance 2. Accommodation Support Organizers will arrange accommodation in Antalya for the full duration of the summit. Where possible, fellows will stay in single-occupancy rooms, although participants should be comfortable with shared accommodation arrangements if necessary. 3. Meal and Stipend Support Selected participants will receive a financial stipend to help cover: Meals Daily incidental expenses Local transportation needs 4. Press Accreditation Assistance The fellowship team will facilitate: Press accreditation processes COP31 logistical coordination On-ground reporting support 5. Visa Reimbursement While participants are responsible for securing their own visas, visa-related costs will be reimbursed by the fellowship organizers. Editorial Training and Journalism Mentorship One of the strongest aspects of the CCMP Fellowship is the extensive editorial support and mentorship provided to fellows before and during the summit. Participants will benefit from: Pre-COP Training Before traveling to Türkiye, fellows will participate in: Virtual workshops Orientation sessions Reporting strategy discussions Climate policy briefings On-the-Ground Mentorship During COP31, fellows will receive: Daily editorial briefings One-on-one mentorship Story development guidance Access to expert interviews Technical reporting assistance The fellowship also provides access to collaboration tools, reporting resources, and communication platforms to support participants throughout the conference. Networking and Global Exposure Opportunities Fellows will gain valuable networking opportunities with: International journalists Climate experts Policymakers Civil society leaders Researchers Environmental advocates Participants may also have opportunities to: Moderate events Speak at side sessions Join media networking mixers Participate in local excursions and cohort-building activities Who Can Apply? The fellowship is open to journalists working across multiple media formats, including: Print journalism Online media Television Radio Multimedia storytelling Digital journalism Eligible Applicants Include: Staff reporters Freelance journalists Independent climate reporters Journalists affiliated with established media houses Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet several important requirements to be considered. Applicants Must: Be professional journalists reporting from eligible low- and middle-income countries Have never attended a UNFCCC climate COP in person before Be available to attend the full two weeks of COP31 in Antalya Have strong English-language communication skills Demonstrate previous experience covering climate-related topics Submit a detailed reporting plan for COP31 coverage Commit to participating in all fellowship activities Required Application Materials Applicants must prepare and submit the following: 1. Updated Resume or CV A current professional resume detailing journalism experience. 2. Video Introduction Applicants must submit a video of no more than two minutes explaining: What they plan to report on during COP31 Why their reporting matters Their climate journalism interests 3. Letter of Support Applicants must provide a signed letter from: An editor Producer Supervisor Supporting media outlet The letter must confirm that the applicant’s stories will be published or broadcast. Freelancers are eligible but must still secure publication commitments from media organizations. 4. Climate Reporting Samples Applicants must provide at least three published climate-related journalism samples produced between: May 15, 2025 and June 15, 2026 Stories may be submitted in any language but must include a short English synopsis. Judging Criteria Applications will be assessed based on several important factors. Reviewers will prioritize applicants who: Demonstrate strong climate journalism experience Present clear and specific story ideas Show understanding of climate policy issues Have realistic and impactful reporting plans Maintain active relationships with media organizations Can produce multiple stories during the fellowship The organizers will also aim to build a diverse cohort representing different: Countries Regions Media formats Journalistic perspectives AI Transparency Requirement Applicants should note that the fellowship organizers require transparency regarding the use of generative AI tools during the application process. Failure to disclose AI-assisted content may result in disqualification. The fellowship organizers also reserve the right to disqualify applicants involved in unethical professional conduct, including plagiarism or misleading submissions. Story Production Expectations Selected fellows are expected to produce multiple stories during the conference. Stories can include: News articles Video reports Radio segments Multimedia content Social media storytelling Investigative features Participants retain editorial freedom in their reporting while adhering to professional journalism standards established by the fellowship. Important Travel and Safety Information Participation in the fellowship requires compliance with all Türkiye travel and entry requirements. The organizers will continuously assess: Health conditions Travel safety Event-related risks Participation remains voluntary, and fellows must ensure they can travel legally and safely to Türkiye. Application Deadline Applications for the COP31 CCMP Reporting Fellowship close on: June 17, 2026 at 11:59 PM (Pacific/Niue Time) Late applications will not be accepted. Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid technical issues. Notification Timeline Successful applicants are expected to receive decisions in: Early September 2026 How to Apply Applicants must apply through the official Earth Journalism Network application platform. Click here for more and to apply Why Journalists Should Apply This fellowship offers an extraordinary opportunity for journalists to: Report from one of the world’s most important climate conferences Gain direct exposure to international climate negotiations Strengthen climate reporting expertise Expand professional networks Access global experts and policymakers Build credibility in environmental journalism Produce impactful climate stories for local audiences For journalists passionate about climate accountability, sustainability, environmental justice, and global policy reporting, the COP31 CCMP Fellowship represents a career-defining opportunity. For more global fellowship opportunities click here The post COP31 Climate Change Media Partnership Fellowship 2026 in Türkiye for Journalists from Developing Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
